title: oxysepala lewisensis
intro: Oxysepala lewisensis, also known as Lewis's oxysepala, is a plant species in the family Gentianaceae. It is a flowering plant native to New Zealand, which has been used for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. In herbal preparations, Oxysepala lewisensis is often used in the form of infusions or decoctions, and can also be found in some commercial herbal supplements.
